title: Continuation: network-diagnose hardening + narrow worker-only recovery workflow

sha_binding.verified_source_note: Continuation of PR #1258, frozen at the T1 bounce limit (4 CHANGES_REQUIRED verdicts) per PM direction. Same already-reviewed content carried forward on a fresh branch from current main. This revision additionally replaces the RestartCount-only manual-restart verification signal with StartedAt (see restart_signal_fix below) and restores test:ops wiring.

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Requires t1-approved label and a Griff-authored pm-verdict/v1 APPROVED comment bound to the exact head before merge.

r_level.command: pnpm exec tsx scripts/ci/r-level-check.ts --base origin/main --head HEAD

workflow_safety_review.note: Same read-only/no-mutation safety review already passed across #1258's review rounds, unchanged in this continuation:

checks.check: Only SSH commands executed are read-only: getent, dig, curl, openssl s_client, docker network ls/inspect, docker logs, ip route/link, sudo iptables -L / ufw status (best-effort)
No docker restart/stop/rm/exec-with-write, no sed/tee/> against .env.production or any config file, no queue/DB write commands
Secret values are never echoed -- only hostnames/ports parsed out of URL-style values, DB test is a bare TCP connect with no credentials sent
ops-worker-recovery.yml is workflow_dispatch-only -- no automatic/scheduled execution, no trigger on push/PR events, this PR does not dispatch it

runtime_proof.note: Live smoke run per T1 policy; this lane changes only GitHub Actions workflow files, a test file, and package.json's test list -- no production code path or migration.

restart_signal_fix.finding: Codex P2: RestartCount-only gating is unreliable for an operator-initiated docker restart, since Docker's RestartCount reflects only restart-policy-triggered restarts (on-failure/always), not a manual `docker restart` invocation.
restart_signal_fix.fix: Replaced the gating check with the container's .State.StartedAt timestamp (captured pre/post as PRE_STARTED_AT/POST_STARTED_AT); PASS requires both non-empty and pre != post. RestartCount is still captured/logged for audit purposes only and no longer gates PASS/FAIL.
restart_signal_fix.regression_test: scripts/ops/worker-recovery-workflow.test.ts: 'worker recovery: manual-restart verification uses StartedAt, not RestartCount, as the gating signal'

abort_check_fail_closed_fix.finding: Codex P2 (fresh @codex review at head 8f8cf3a5): the abort-check piped docker logs' merged stderr output directly into grep -c, so a docker logs failure (missing container, daemon down, permission denied) yielded RECENT_502=0 and silently took the 'no recent evidence, safe no-op' exit-0 path instead of failing closed.
abort_check_fail_closed_fix.fix: docker logs output is now captured into RECENT_502_RAW via plain command substitution (not a pipe), with its own exit code captured separately as DOCKER_LOGS_EXIT immediately afterward. A non-zero exit fails the script closed with an explicit ::error:: annotation before RECENT_502 is computed.
abort_check_fail_closed_fix.regression_test: scripts/ops/worker-recovery-workflow.test.ts: 'worker recovery: abort-check fails closed if docker logs itself fails, instead of treating an inspection failure as no-evidence-found'

acceptance_criteria.network_diagnose_hardening_carried_forwa: PASS -- DB/pooler key discovery + curl-or-node HTTPS fallback

known_gaps: Docker daemon events window covers only 7 days.
Deploy-timestamp correlation done at the analysis layer, not inside the SSH probe.

File Scope Lock (advisory, not a required status check) fails on a ghost docs/06_status/lanes/UTV2-1560.json manifest left on main from merged PR #1256 (status never advanced past 'started'); fixing it requires a main-side ops:lane-close --repair-merged reconciliation, outside this lane branch's scope.
Readiness Regression Gate (advisory, not a required status check) fails on a repo-wide, pre-existing readiness-score.json ledger unrelated to this lane.
